commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7feb623800789f7dd5d9a2431900e065be3cb749)
commands.review_summary:
The PR clamps compaction reserve settings to at most half the resolved context window and changes context-overflow recovery copy to tell users to lower an oversized reserve.

PR surface: Source +41, Tests -8. Total +33 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the arithmetic path in source: current main allows a 70k reserve floor on a 120k context window, leaving only 50k for the compacted prompt. I did not establish a live Telegram or real-agent reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer compatibility/rebase review; automation cannot supply the external proof for this PR.

target.title: Guard compaction reserve against prompt starvation
target.body:
## Summary
- cap Pi compaction reserve to at most half of the resolved context window
- lower an already-too-large reserve during Pi settings application instead of preserving prompt starvation
- make the context-overflow recovery hint warn when the configured reserve is too high for the model window

## Background
A Telegram session on a 120k-token model had `agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokensFloor=70000`. After compaction, the prompt still needed about 54k tokens, but the 70k reserve left only 50k input budget, so every retry overflowed and the user-facing recovery hint incorrectly suggested increasing the reserve.

## Test plan
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-settings.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
target.author: cbcampos
